perturbation.original_text: The Fees for the Project shall be $279,000.00 Payment Schedule: Client shall pay Developer: 20% Deposit ($55,800.00) to be paid at the start of the project. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #1. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #2. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #3. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #4.
perturbation.changed_text: The Fees for the Project shall be $279,000.00 Payment Schedule: Client shall remit to Vendor: 20% Deposit ($55,800.00) to be paid at the start of the project. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #1. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #2. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #3. 20% ($55,800.00) to be paid upon completion of Milestone #4.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Developer' to 'Vendor' introduces ambiguity. While the initial statement of work defines the parties as 'Developer' and 'Client', using 'Vendor' here creates confusion about which entity is receiving the payments. This is contradicting the initial definitions in the original document and creates uncertainty. The change will create uncertainty in who is the payor of the project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Statement of Work is issued to and made part of that Software Development Agreement executed by and between Developer and Client on the Effective Date (the Agreement). Any word or phrase not otherwise defined in this Appendix will have the meaning ascribed in the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Developer' to 'Vendor' introduces ambiguity regarding the payee. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'Developer' and 'Client' are the defined parties in the agreement, and any undefined terms will have the meaning ascribed in the agreement. By introducing 'Vendor', the modified text creates a direct conflict with the established definitions, causing uncertainty about who should receive payments, and this is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: New users can select Sign-up and go through the quick and easy sign-up process. The application will ask for the user's: § Email § Password / Confirm Password § First Name § Last Name § Phone Number § Birthday
perturbation.changed_text: New patrons can select Sign-up and go through the quick and easy sign-up process. The application will ask for the user's: § Email § Password / Confirm Password § First Name § Last Name § Phone Number § Birthday
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'users' to 'patrons' introduces a conflict in terminology. The document consistently refers to 'users' in the context of the application and website functionality. By using 'patrons' in this specific section, the contract creates uncertainty if these terms are interchangeable or refer to different classes of individuals interacting with the Pelican Delivers platform. This creates uncertainty as to the sign up process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Website (Customer Portal) Users will be able to go to the Pelican Delivers front-end website and easily search, browse, and order cannabis. Before a user can gain access to the website, they must first verify their birthday (users must be 21 and over to even view the website).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the term 'users' with 'patrons' in the sign-up process description. The contradicted text uses 'users' when describing website access and age verification. The explanation states that this creates uncertainty as to the sign up process. Since the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, it leads to conflicting obligations, which falls under Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, a change that can lead to contradictory obligations regarding who can access the website and how.

perturbation.original_text: Once payment is confirmed users may proceed to completing their order · Age / ID Verification o After payment is confirmed users must upload their government issued ID and a selfie o Trulioo will verify if the issued ID is valid and if the selfie matches the ID o Once verification is completed the user will be able to complete the order
perturbation.changed_text: Once payment is confirmed customers may proceed to completing their order · Age / ID Verification o After payment is confirmed patrons must upload their government issued ID and a selfie o Trulioo will verify if the issued ID is valid and if the selfie matches the ID o Once verification is completed the customer will be able to complete the order
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'users', 'customers', and 'patrons' are used inconsistently in this section. The initial sentence uses 'customers', then 'patrons' is used, followed by 'customer' again. This introduces ambiguity as to who needs to provide the ID and creates doubt about whether these terms are interchangeable or have different implications within the context of the order completion process. It's unclear if the ID verification process applies to all or only certain groups.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Website (Customer Portal) Users will be able to go to the Pelican Delivers front-end website and easily search, browse, and order cannabis. Before a user can gain access to the website, they must first verify their birthday (users must be 21 and over to even view the website).
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'users' consistently, referring to individuals accessing the website and completing orders. The changed text introduces 'customers' and 'patrons', which contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' that specifically refers to 'Website (Customer Portal) Users'. The inconsistent use of these terms creates ambiguity about who the ID verification process applies to, directly conflicting with the contradicted text's clear reference to 'users' and their age verification requirements before accessing the website. This violates the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because multiple terms are used interchangeably without clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty about which terms apply to the ID verification process.
